hypothesis: Augmenting the DPO reward with a high-temperature B2 stability constraint—requiring ≥25% B2 volume fraction at T ≥ 1373 K—will increase, by at least 20% relative to the original DPO setup, the fraction of LLaMA-3.1-8B generations that satisfy room-temperature (373 K) B2 presence, while maintaining coverage recall ≥0.98 and unique pairs @100 ≥0.95.
